highlights.title: Alphabet, Amazon Outpace Meta in AI During Earnings Bonanza
highlights.description: A frenzied day of earnings reports offered a glimpse at how some of the world’s biggest tech companies are doing in artificial intelligence. The upshot: Alphabet Inc.’s Google is seeing a clear payoff from its AI spending, while Meta Platforms Inc. is lagging behind.

highlights.title: SoftBank reportedly weighs $100 billion valuation for new AI and robotics spinout in potential U.S. IPO
highlights.description: SoftBank Group is planning to create and list a standalone artificial intelligence and robotics company, coined "Roze" in the U.S.

highlights.title: Amazon, Meta join fight to end Google Pay, PhonePe dominance in India
highlights.description: PhonePe and Google Pay command 80% of India's UPI instant payments network. Rivals are set to meet with regulators to lobby for restrictions.

highlights.title: Sources: Anthropic could raise a new $50B round at a valuation of $900B
highlights.description: The maker of Claude has received multiple pre-emptive offers at valuations in the $850 billion to $900 billion range, according to sources familiar with the matter.

highlights.title: SoftBank’s $40 Billion Loan for OpenAI Stake Draws More Banks
highlights.description: Banks that recently signed a $40 billion bridge loan with SoftBank Group Corp. for its investment in US tech giant OpenAI have attracted more lenders to the deal in syndication, people familiar with the matter said.

highlights.title: Investors still trust Google more than Meta when it comes to spending their money on AI
highlights.description: Alphabet and Meta both boosted their capex guidance in their first-quarter earnings reports, but Wall Street viewed one much more favorably than the other.

highlights.title: SoftBank is creating a robotics company that builds data centers — and already eyeing a $100B IPO
highlights.description: You need infrastructure to build AI a and robots, but apparently you also need AI and robots to build infrastructure.

highlights.title: Elon Musk’s worst enemy in court is Elon Musk
highlights.description:
Just a simple country CEO who was being tricked by that sophisticated lawyer | Image: Cath Virginia / The Verge, Getty Images	

About five hours into Elon Musk's testimony, I typed the following sentence into my notes: "I have never been more sympathetic to Sam Altman in my life."
Musk's direct testimony was an improvement over yesterday - even if his lawyer kept asking leading questions to cue him in how to answer. But that memory was immediately obliterated by an absolutely miserable cross-examination. For hours, Musk refused to answer yes or no questions with yes or no, occasionally "forgot" things he'd testified to in the morning, and scolded defense lawyer William Savitt. I watched a few jury members glance at each other. During one testy exchange, one woman wa …
Read the full story at The Verge.

highlights.title: OpenAI Meets Key AI Computing Capacity Goal Ahead of Schedule
highlights.description: OpenAI has met a key milestone for securing AI capacity in the US several years ahead of schedule, boosting the startup’s ambitious plans for data center expansion.

highlights.title: Anthropic Plan to Expand Mythos Access Is Opposed by White House
highlights.description: The White House opposes a plan by Anthropic PBC to expand access to its Mythos AI model, an administration official said Wednesday night.

ai.title: Startup Bringing Brains to AI Aims for $2.5 Billion Valuation
ai.description: Thomas Reardon, who led the work on Meta Platforms Inc.’s Neural Band, is raising funds for a new startup called Flourish to develop energy-efficient artificial intelligence, according to people familiar with the matter.

ai.title: Nvidia just invested in the AI legal startup that's splashing Jude Law ads everywhere
ai.description: Swedish startup Legora has raised more than $800 million in the past 12 months, and the latest deal values it at $5.6 billion.

ai.title: Samsung profit surges over eightfold to beat estimates as AI boom fuels memory chip crunch
ai.description: Samsung Electronics reported an over eightfold increase in first-quarter operating profits, hitting a new record and beating analysts' estimates.

ai.title: Samsung Beats Estimates After Chip Sales Defy War Fears
ai.description: Samsung Electronics Co. earned a far stronger-than-expected eight-fold leap in quarterly profit, underscoring robust demand for AI memory chips in the face of markets roiled by war in the Middle East.  (Source: Bloomberg)

ai.title: Samsung, SK Hynix Payouts to Fuel Rate Hikes, Mirae Asset Says
ai.description: The extraordinary wealth generated from South Korean chipmakers will drive spending and inflation, spurring the nation’s central bank to raise interest rates at least three times over the coming year, according to Mirae Asset Global Investments.

ai.title: Microsoft delivers a promising quarter but can't shake the software fears
ai.description: With plenty of puts and takes, it's not surprising to see the stock oscillating between modest gains and losses in after-hours trading.

ai.title: Murata Tops Profit Estimates on Rising AI Data Center Demand
ai.description: Murata Manufacturing Co. reported fourth-quarter earnings that beat analyst estimates, fueled by robust demand from artificial intelligence data center builders.

ai.title: Microsoft lifts 2026 AI spend by $25 billion to cover component price rises
Europe’s Embattled Software Firms Rise Above AI, Iran Challenges
ai.description: Europe’s software companies are doing better than expected this earnings season, brushing off fears around artificial intelligence and the impact of the Iran war on business confidence.

ai.title: Mphasis CEO on Business Strategy, AI Investments
ai.description: Nitin Rakesh, CEO at Indian IT firm Mphasis, discusses the company's business strategy and its investments in AI. He speaks with Haidi Stroud-Watts on Bloomberg: The Asia Trade. (Source: Bloomberg)

ai.title: Apple China highlights Apple Watch health and rescue stories in campaign with podcast tie-in
ai.description:
Apple launched a new campaign in China called “Thankfully, I was wearing it” (“还好戴着它”), which includes a special podcast episode in which three users share their stories. Here are the details.
more…

ai.title: US Big Tech Ratchets Up AI Spending Past $700 Billion This Year
ai.description: The biggest US tech firms now plan to spend as much as $725 billion this year on capital expenditures, primarily on AI data center equipment.

business.title: SoftBank reportedly weighs $100 billion valuation for new AI and robotics spinout in potential U.S. IPO
business.description: SoftBank Group is planning to create and list a standalone artificial intelligence and robotics company, coined "Roze" in the U.S.

business.title: Meta’s Reality Labs lost over $4 billion in first quarter
business.description: In Meta's first-quarter earnings report, the company said Reality Labs recorded an operating loss of over $4 billion while bringing in $402 million in sales.

business.title: Microsoft calls for $190 billion in 2026 capital spending on soaring memory prices
business.description: Microsoft's forecast on revenue and operating margin was light, but the company sees $190 billion in 2026 capital spending, well above Wall Street's estimate.

business.title: Alphabet ups 2026 capex to as much as $190 billion, expects to 'significantly increase' in 2027
business.description: Alphabet reported first-quarter earnings, posting booming cloud revenue and upping its full-year capex spend to as much as $190 billion.

business.title: Amazon earnings beat expectations with strong cloud growth
business.description: Revenue in Amazon's cloud computing segment expanded 28% year over year, topping analysts' estimates.

robotics.title: SoftBank is creating a robotics company that builds data centers — and already eyeing a $100B IPO
robotics.description: You need infrastructure to build AI a and robots, but apparently you also need AI and robots to build infrastructure.

ev_autonomous.title: Meta stock drops on quarterly results as 'internet disruptions' in Iran drag down user numbers
ev_autonomous.description: Meta beat on revenue, but showed disappointing user numbers and its capital expenditures were below estimates.

chips_cloud.title: Google to sell its TPUs to some customers, who also fancy big-G GPUs
Amazon’s cloud business is surging — and so is its capital spending
chips_cloud.description: The e-commerce giant is making more money than expected from AWS but it's also spending a lot, and will continue to do so in the near term, its chief executive said.

chips_cloud.title: Microsoft levels up Azure Local to make it fit for large-scale sovereign clouds
